sections.paragraph:
πέπειρος, ον (Soph. et al.) ripe (Theophr., CP 3, 6, 9; Artem. 2, 25; JosAs 2:19) τὸ πέπειρον ripeness (Herm. Wr. 1, 17: Rtzst., Poim. 333) εἰς πέπειρον καταντᾶν come to ripeness of the fruit of the vine (cp. Gen 40:10) 1 Cl 23:4.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Rtzst., Poim. Rtzst., Poim. = Poimandres

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 795
